data.location: 3940 Buford Hwy NE Suite B101, Duluth, GA 30096
data.status: Targeting opening in a few weeks (pending permits)
data.short_description: Expanded standalone Latin-Japanese fusion restaurant with ~50 seats, a sushi bar and separate drink bar. Owned by Wilson Giovanny Perez and Melina Castan. Menu includes sushi, ceviche, tacos, and Peruvian-influenced dishes.

data.location: 5380 Peachtree Industrial Blvd Unit 100, Norcross, GA 30071
data.short_description: Coffee shop centered on music and performance with events like poetry slams, karaoke, and open mic nights. Owned by Malvina McNeill. Menu includes espresso drinks, pour-overs, cold brew, and pastries.
Casual taqueria focused on authentic Mexican cuisine with dine-in, takeout, and delivery. Owned by Brayan Romero. Menu centers on street tacos and traditional Mexican dishes.

Family-run Peruvian restaurant with full-service format and from-scratch cooking. Owned by Mayra Chipoco and family. Planned seven days a week with menu items including pan con chicharrón and chicha morada.

data.location: Address not specified (article text cut off)
data.short_description: Article text was cut off before details for this business were provided. No further information available.

data.status: Targeting a grand opening at the beginning of May
data.short_description: Pool hall-focused venue with five leagues, a full pro shop, darts, a golf game, a bar area, and lounge space. Owned by Scott Stoltz. Food service will start in a limited format with cold finger foods, snacks, and sandwiches.

data.store_name: SKOL Brewing Company (Valhalla Gaming Lounge expansion)
data.short_description: SKOL Brewing Company is expanding with a ~30,000 SF gaming lounge called Valhalla Gaming Lounge, adding 40+ gaming options, live music areas, and a new commercial kitchen. Led by Gray Lane and Drew Tull. The existing 5,000 SF brewpub has operated since 2025.

data.status: Targeting an opening in the coming weeks
data.short_description: New pizza restaurant and live music venue from musicians Buddy Black and John Forbes with capacity for ~300 guests indoors plus outdoor seating. Menu centers on New York-style pizza with a full bar and regular live performances.

data.event_date: Late summer, or by October at the latest
data.short_description: New cookie concept shop near City Hall in a walkable area, owned by Brisma Fermin. Targeting a late summer opening at the latest by October.
Fast-casual barbecue operation with a drive-thru in a location formerly known for smoked meats (Mr. K's BBQ). Owned by Robert Rukab and his partner Zee.
Second Potbelly in the Jacksonville market for owners Patrick and Leah Pendergast. Their first Jacksonville location is set to debut in April at 9908 Old Baymeadows Road.
First Jacksonville Potbelly location for owners Patrick and Leah Pendergast, debuting ahead of their second Beach Boulevard location.

Bakery-cafe on the lower level of a 24-unit apartment building, owned by franchisees Rishy and Quint Studer. Will feature fresh-milled wheat breads, a counter-service format, and ~45 indoor seats plus patio.
Pool hall concept with five leagues, pro shop, darts, golf game, bar, and lounge. Owned by Scott Stoltz. (Duplicate entry from Article 5 roundup referencing Article 2.)

data.location: 811 S Main Street, Suite 206, Unit 811-106, Rolesville, NC 27571
data.status: Targeting a late spring to early summer opening
data.short_description: Beer garden-style pub with ~2,000 SF indoors plus outdoor space, owned by Dan Colby and Liza David. Will feature ~20 local craft beers on tap, live music, yard games, and a fire pit; no food served directly on-site.

data.location: 2286 W International Speedway Blvd, Daytona Beach, FL 32114
data.short_description: Korean-based franchise offering Korean fried chicken in a fast-casual format with seating for 50–70 guests. Owned by Surya Rao and his wife Mary Rao. Menu includes wings, boneless options, kimchi, tteokbokki, and chicken wraps.

data.event_date: End of April or early May
data.status: Aiming to open by the end of April
data.short_description: Casual full-service Chinese restaurant specializing in traditional Peking duck and handmade soup dumplings, with seating for ~150. Chef trained by Michelin-star-recognized Quanjude; menu also includes zha jiang noodles, Mongolian beef, and Chengdu chicken.

Bakery-cafe franchise owned by Rishy and Quint Studer, offering fresh-milled breads, breakfast sandwiches, soups, salads, and coffee with ~45 indoor seats and patio seating. Located on the lower level of the Studers' 24-unit apartment building alongside an adjacent marketplace.

data.status: Targeting a early May to mid-May opening, pending final city inspections
data.short_description: Full-service Dominican restaurant with ~1,500 SF and seating for ~40, owned by Rene Perez and Brenda Reyes. Menu features traditional Dominican dishes including stewed chicken, roasted pork, oxtail, mofongo, and Dominican-style burgers (chimi).

data.location: 20601 Torrence Chapel Rd Suite 106, Cornelius, NC 28031
data.short_description: Third location of Your Mom's Donuts, owned by Dustin and Colleen Wanemacher, taking over the former Sweet Cakes Bakery space (~1,400 SF). Will offer specialty square donuts and a coffee program with limited indoor and outdoor seating.

data.status: Opening date has not been announced
data.short_description: First street-level retail location for The Jugo Bar, owned by William Fulton, offering fresh-made juices, smoothies, smoothie bowls, algae-based offerings, and rooibos teas in a ~3,000 SF space. Business is ~85% ready and awaiting final inspections.

New deli in the former Smoothie King space, owned by Sarah and Barrett Dabbs, featuring hot and cold sandwiches, soups, and salads with a drive-thru. GC has finished the remodel and the team is currently decorating and designing the interior.

data.event_date: Soft opening in coming weeks; grand opening end of May or early June
data.status: Aiming for a soft opening in the coming weeks and a grand opening toward the end of May or early June
data.short_description: Third location for owner Samuel Bustamante's nutrition shop group, offering healthy shakes, energy refreshers, fruit bowls, waffles, and donuts. Construction is already underway including bar buildout, painting, and lighting.

data.status: Targeting a late May to early June opening
data.short_description: Upscale modern Indian restaurant from Anish Nair with ~113 seats, a mezzanine for private dining, and a 14-seat bar. Menu centers on small plates and shareable dishes drawing from regional Indian flavors with a contemporary approach.

Craft distillery and tasting room from Mitch Patel, ~8,000 SF, focused exclusively on vodka with guided tours, copper still production, and cocktail tastings. No restaurant component; expected to operate around major downtown events.

data.status: Planning a new location; targeting a summer 2026 opening
data.short_description: Fast-casual Greek yogurt concept joining the Sundial redevelopment near the St. Petersburg Pier. Will offer fresh and frozen yogurt bowls, yogurt-based salads, and a self-serve frozen yogurt bar with customizable toppings.

Crisp Kitchen & Cocktails has reopened at a new location ~100 feet from its former Latta Arcade space, with lunch service underway since March 5. Dinner and cocktail service featuring a Japanese highball machine is expected to launch in early April.

Full-service neighborhood sports bar with ~90 indoor seats and 35 patio seats, owned by Tim McGivern. Menu centers on wings, burgers, wraps, and casual comfort food; described as the state's first Wings Etc. location.

Restaurant-first bar and grill with ~100 indoor seats plus a 40-seat patio, owned by Rob Burton. Menu features scratch-made ribeye steaks, lobster rolls, crab cakes, Cajun pasta, and half-pound burgers.

data.location: 257 North Cattleman Drive, Unit 81, Sarasota, FL 34243
data.short_description: Scratch-kitchen concept with indoor-outdoor layout, seating for ~145 guests, and globally influenced dishes spanning Indian, Asian, Mexican, and Italian flavors. Signature items include cheddar scallion biscuits and weekend "cinna biscuit."
Cajun seafood restaurant and market with 60–70 seats, owned by Tim Le. Menu includes crawfish boils, blue crabs, oysters, po' boys, and gumbo.
Roman-style pizza concept with ~50 seats, 72-hour fermented dough pizzas, star-shaped pies with ricotta, and burrata boards. Owned by Karif Cohen; founded by Renato Viola.
French bakery and brunch café with ~50 seats, owned by Fanette and Patrick Maquet. Menu includes croque monsieur, quiche, onion soup, croissants, and baguette sandwiches; open daily 8 a.m.–2:30 p.m.

To-go barbecue concept evolving from a food truck, owned by Candis Haney and pitmaster Michael Wheeler. Menu features brisket, ribs, meatloaf, baked beans, and cobblers; open Tuesday–Sunday until sold out.

data.location: 6813 Buffalo Rd., Unit 103, Palmetto, FL (zip code not specified)
data.short_description: Fast-casual boba tea shop with grab-and-go service. Menu includes milk teas, fruit teas, smoothies, brown sugar boba, and dalgona coffee. Article text cut off before further details.

Supper club-style dining with a vintage-inspired design and mezzanine lounge. Offers contemporary American dishes including steaks, pork chops, short ribs, and house-made desserts. Owned by Malachy Noone with partners Kyle Miller and Frederick Hancock.

A 2,700 sq ft restaurant with ~66 seats and hybrid counter/table service; first North Carolina location for the brand. Menu features brisket, pulled pork, spare ribs, smoked chicken, and Carolina vinegar-style barbecue. Owned by Abby Haywood and Evangeline Smith.

Speakeasy-style bourbon bar and cigar lounge with hidden entrance and upscale interior. Menu includes bourbon-focused cocktails, Neapolitan pizza, and small plates. Owned by Ralph Mensah and Michael Thomas.
Coffee shop, bookstore, and vinyl concept with rotating curated inventory and workspace vibe. Planned daily hours of 8 a.m.–4 p.m. Owned by Jermaine Bantum.
Article text is cut off after the address and status; no further details available. Located in Durham, NC.

data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.short_description: After years of mobile service, Cheez'd and Confuzed is opening a stationary location serving indulgent cheesy sandwiches from a converted 1964 Shasta Airflyte camper, supported by an onsite commissary kitchen. Lunch service will run 11 a.m.–3 p.m. with breakfast and dinner planned as the team grows.

data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.short_description: A new food truck concept from owner Daniel Ray focusing on steak and frites, primarily serving hanger steaks over fries with sauces like chimichurri. The truck will be co-located at the same Ball Ground address as Cheez'd and Confuzed.

data.location: 2110 Powers Ferry Rd Suite 120, Atlanta, GA 30339
data.short_description: First permanent brick-and-mortar location for this vegan concept from mother-daughter team Tanetta Ellzey and Iyonna Poags, after two to three years as a food truck and pop-up. Capacity is up to 54 guests with indoor and outdoor patio seating, open Monday–Saturday, 10 a.m.–10 p.m.
Brewery led by partners Alan A. McDonald, Kevin J. Wright, and Kevin Bower targeting an April 1 opening in Southport, NC. Located in Brunswick County.

data.status: Set to open with a soft opening weekend beginning April 3rd
data.short_description: Community-focused cafe and market from owner Leslie Allred in Beaufort, NC (Carteret County). Final inventory and product deliveries are underway ahead of opening.
Casual deli and market concept from owner Brandon Brock targeting a May opening near Bailey Park in Winston-Salem, NC (Forsyth County).

data.status: Targeting an opening in the coming months
data.short_description: Ramen and noodle restaurant from husband-and-wife team Chong Li and Jessica Zhao targeting an opening in the coming months in Winston-Salem, NC (Forsyth County).

data.status: Targeting an opening in the first week of April
data.short_description: Takes over the former Plank Road Steakhouse space in Farmville, NC (Pitt County) with a refreshed concept and updated approach. Targeting an opening in the first week of April.
Luxury cocktail lounge from owners Kenny Patel and Maulik Parikh focusing on craft cocktails, mocktails, beer, and wine in a high-end setting in Apex, NC (Wake County). Article text is cut off.

data.event_date: Soft opening Thursday; grand opening on the 30th
data.status: Planning a soft opening Thursday followed by a grand opening celebration on the 30th
data.short_description: Soul food restaurant from Bobby and Christy Pressley, transitioning from catering and a mall setting to a standalone space in Whitesburg, GA (Carroll County).

data.location: 15 Thomas Grace Annex Ln #200, Sharpsburg, GA 30277
data.status: Targeting a summer opening pending permits and licenses
data.short_description: Second location of Frida's Grill and Bar from owner Teodoro Reyes Llamas, targeting a summer opening in Sharpsburg, GA (Coweta County), pending permits and licenses.

data.location: 11105 State Bridge Rd Ste 360, Johns Creek, GA 30022
data.status: Fully built out and awaiting final city approvals
data.short_description: Late-night, indulgent menu concept centered on sandwiches, sides, and comfort food from owner Videsh Kumar in Johns Creek, GA (Fulton County). Restaurant is fully built out and awaiting final city approvals.

data.location: 290 Nicholas Pkwy NW Unit 5, Cape Coral, FL 33991
data.short_description: Fast-casual Latin bowl concept focused on takeout and delivery with limited seating in Cape Coral, FL. Offers customizable bowls with proteins like chicken, salmon, steak, and lamb, plus sides like plantains. Owned by Naylin Zamora.

Fast-casual coastal Mexican concept with a build-your-own service line and about 45 seats in Cape Coral, FL. Family- and veteran-owned by Antoinette, Phil, and Vito Basso; dine-in, takeout, and pickup window available.

data.location: 122 Chiquita Blvd. S, Unit 3, Cape Coral, FL 33991
data.short_description: Fast-casual Dominican restaurant with a service line setup and about 24 indoor seats in Cape Coral, FL. Open for breakfast, lunch, and dinner; owned by Arthur Sanchez with Chef Arlene Ovalles.

data.location: 1189 SW Pine Island Rd., Cape Coral, FL 33991
data.status: Targeting late May or early June opening
data.short_description: Dual-lane drive-thru coffee concept with walk-up windows and limited outdoor seating in Cape Coral, FL. Menu features custom coffee drinks, white chocolate mochas, caramel drinks, breakfast sandwiches, and baked goods. Article text is cut off.

data.status: Targeting an opening by late summer, or at the very least by October
data.short_description: Quick-service cookie shop with service window and outdoor seating near City Hall in Miramar, FL, from owner Brisma Fermin. Features an open kitchen and rotating monthly menu of cookies along with milkshakes, coffee, and cookie sandwiches.

data.location: 10108 SW Discovery Way, Port St. Lucie, FL 34987
data.status: Targeting a soft opening around April 1 and a grand opening around April 20
data.short_description: Second location of Swift Grill from owner Vlad Bradescu in Port St. Lucie, FL, featuring a hybrid fast-casual and dine-in tapas concept seating ~121 guests. Menu is shawarma-focused with Romanian, Greek, and Middle Eastern tapas influences, plus a full liquor menu with THC cocktails.

Kush Wynwood is reopening after a two-year closure due to extensive building renovations on the nearly 100-year-old structure. The full-service restaurant from owners Matthew and Priscilla Kuscher will seat ~40 indoors, with a menu focused on burgers, sandwiches, and salads with locally sourced ingredients.

data.location: 6901 Collins Ave, CU1, Miami Beach, FL 33141
data.status: Not specified (exact opening date has not been announced)
data.short_description: Fast-casual European cafe and espresso bar from co-owners Efthymios Paliouras and Ioannis Sotiropoulos, marking their 27th location in Miami Beach. Offers ~100 total seats, a full kitchen with 20+ breakfast options, and an espresso program using Illy coffee; open 7 a.m.–9 p.m. daily.

Fast-casual barbecue operation with a drive-thru and limited indoor seating (~12 guests) from owner Robert Rukab and partner Zee, taking over the former Mr. K's BBQ space. Menu centers on pit-cooked barbecue including chicken, ribs, macaroni and cheese, and classic sides.

First location of Jatski's Ice Cream from owners Amy and Bill Laakkonen, serving house-made, scratch-produced ice cream in a 1,200 sq ft space with seating for up to 12 guests. Flavors include honey vanilla, coconut, chocolate velvet, and rum raisin; also offers sundaes, milkshakes, waffle cones, and Finnish specialty items.

data.event_date: Week of April 20 / by end of April
data.status: Opening expected by the end of April
data.short_description: Fourth Tallahassee location of the 40-year-old locally owned sandwich and salad shop, passed down through the Ogden family. This location will be the first Hopkins' Eatery with a grill, bar seating, and alcohol service, with expected capacity of 75–99 seats including outdoor seating.
